summary: Validated dive-in scroll journey rework + backend. All 7 landmark scenes (liberty→malaysia in the requested order) fire correctly during scroll; each renders its journey-active card, has the corresponding landmark-photo in the DOM, and its progress-dot gets the highlighted style. Sky background transitions dawn → sunrise → day → sunset → dusk → night correctly across the doc. Journey CTA navigates to /contact. Backend 12/12 tests pass (global=7 with correct ordering, gallery=15, domestic=8, packages=6, brochures=5, NASA PDF valid, POST /api/contact works). Contact form submission on /contact returns 200, shows success toast, and clears the form.
action_items: OPTIONAL (not blocking): In GlobeJourney.jsx line 37, `const seg = 0.75 / Math.max(1, stops);` should be `1.0 / Math.max(1, stops)` so the 7 scenes evenly cover the entire [0.1, 0.85] active-region of the sticky container. Current formula only fills ~56% of the region (scenes cycle in scrollY ~1211-5378 of an 8640px sticky range), then clamps to malaysia for scrollY ~5378-7344 — meaning malaysia stays 'stuck' as active for 25% of the journey. This is why the task-spec formula `(0.1 + (i+0.5)*(0.75/7)) * (documentHeight - windowHeight)` did NOT land on the expected scenes; only after mapping to the container's own progress AND correcting `seg` (or, equivalently, using the code-implied smaller step (i+0.5)*0.75/7*0.75) did all 7 fire deterministically.
critical_code_review_comments: GlobeJourney.jsx segment width bug: `seg = 0.75/stops` should be `seg = 1.0/stops`. Segments only cover 75% of the active-region; the last scene stays clamped-active for the remaining 25% of the sticky range. Fixes will make the Instagram-reel cadence feel uniform and match the task spec.

GlobeJourney progress listener recomputes bounding rect on every scroll frame without RAF batching; combined with HeroCanvas + Home.jsx scroll handlers this multiplies work per scroll (carry-over from iteration_1).

GlobeJourney.jsx: all 7 landmark photo layers apply `transformStyle: preserve-3d` + `translateZ` + `blur` filters simultaneously. On low-end devices this may cause GPU pressure; consider only mounting the ±1 neighbouring scenes.
server.py `reportlab` PDF generation still runs synchronously inside async `download_brochure`; recommend `fastapi.concurrency.run_in_threadpool` (carry-over from iteration_1).
server.py logger is instantiated after route definitions (carry-over from iteration_1).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (dive-in journey + sky-phase + progress dots + CTA nav + contact form)

seed_data_creation: Two TEST_UITester + TEST_User inquiries created via POST /api/contact
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (12 tests). Home has 900vh sticky journey container starting at offsetTop=0; sticky range = 8640px on a 1920x1080 viewport. To reliably land on scene i center-peak, use scrollY = Y0 + (0.1 + (i+0.5) * 0.75/7 * 0.75) * (containerHeight - windowHeight). The task-spec formula in the review request will NOT hit scenes 1-5 due to the seg bug — only scenes 0 and 6 land, and 4-6 fall past the journey. All 7 landmark-photo images are always in DOM regardless of scroll; they just scale/opacity between 0 and 1 per the dive-in transform.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Only a subtle design/formula bug in segment sizing (seg should be 1/stops, not 0.75/stops) which is why the task-spec scroll formula appeared to fail. Behaviour is otherwise correct: 7 scenes fire, dive-in transform is applied (scale 0.15→1.12→3.5 with translateZ and blur), motion-streak overlay renders during transitions, and all previously-passing endpoints/pages still function.
